leadText: Mortal Spin (Japanese: キラースピン Killer Spin) is a damage-dealing Poison-type move introduced in Generation IX.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Mortal Spin inflicts damage and then removes any binding effects and Leech Seed from the user, as well as entry hazards from the user's side of the field (unless the user has fainted as a result of the move, such as from Rough Skin ). It also leaves each target poisoned . In a battle with multiple opponents, it will target all adjacent opponents. Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The following moves are affected by Mortal Spin: Move Type Cat. Bind Normal Physical Fire Spin Fire Special Infestation Bug Special Leech Seed Grass Status Magma Storm Fire Special Sand Tomb Ground Physical Snap Trap Grass Physical Spikes Ground Status Stealth Rock Rock Status Sticky Web Bug Status Toxic Spikes Poison Status Whirlpool Water Special Wrap Normal Physical

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V The user performs a spin attack that can also eliminate the effects of such moves as Bind, Wrap, and Leech Seed. This also poisons opposing Pokémon. ZA The user attacks by spinning about. This leaves targets poisoned and also increases the user's movement speed for a short time.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Mortal Spin's Japanese name, キラースピン Kirā Supin , may be a pun on both キラキラ kirakira (onomatopoeia for glittering) and キラー kirā (killer), like with Glimmora 's Japanese name. In French, Italian, and Chinese, the "glittering" meaning is kept instead of the "killer" meaning.
